title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond at a 3.5% coupon to finance its foreign currency reserves, maturing on 18 February 2029. This was the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced in September 2025, which shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and high transparency.
key_points: US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond launched under RegS/144A format
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
First 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced on 23 September 2025
Issuance programme shift (September 2025) targets two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
why_it_matters: This notice documents the Bank of England's implementation of its updated foreign currency reserves financing strategy. The shift to two annual benchmark issuances with consistent timing and distribution groups represents a structural change in how the BoE accesses dollar funding markets, affecting primary dealer relationships and eurobond market dynamics.
market_structure_context: The notice describes a structural change in BoE reserve financing operations: the shift from irregular issuance to a scheduled twice-yearly benchmark programme with named dealer groups. This affects primary market operations for BoE USD-denominated debt and provides greater predictability for market participants.
notes: This is a factual market notice documenting a sovereign debt issuance. The notice primarily serves as an operational record rather than containing forward-looking policy signals. Note that the legal disclaimers and forward-looking statement boilerplate constitute the majority of the document text; substantive financial terms are limited to the bond parameters and dealer group. Page type correctly classified as market_notice per taxonomy.
